title: frangula californica subsp. crassifolia
intro: Frangula californica subsp. crassifolia, also known as the California buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a laxative and antiseptic due to the presence of anthraquinone compounds. The health benefits of Frangula californica subsp. crassifolia include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of constipation and diarrhea. Frangula californica subsp. crassifolia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract for its medicinal purposes, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it.
